gpu: Intel Mesa Intel Arc B580 (BMG G21)
notes:
The game fails to transition from the initial splash screens (Marvel Logo/Legal) to the main 3D menu. After the intro videos finish, a CTD (Crash to Desktop) occurs instantly. This behavior is persistent across multiple Proton versions and configuration attempts.

Technical Analysis & Reproduction Steps
Driver Initialization: the game correctly identifies the hardware via the xe driver. The launcher and splash screens (2D/Video) render correctly.

The "3D Wall": the crash occurs during the allocation of the first 3D scene. Monitoring via btop and LACT shows 0% GPU utilization during the crash, suggesting a Kernel Mode Driver (KMD) hang or a failure in the Vulkan pipeline state object (PSO) creation.

DX12/VKD3D Incompatibility: the game forces DirectX 12. Attempting to force -dx11 or -d3d11 results in a "No compatible AC adapter found" error, indicating the engine's DX11 path is deprecated or incompatible with the current Xe2 Vulkan entry points.

Vendor Spoofing Failure: Applying dxgi.customVendorId=0x10de (NVIDIA) or 0x1002 (AMD) bypasses the initial "Unsupported Hardware" check but does not prevent the CTD, confirming the issue lies in how Mesa/Xe2 handles specific DX12 descriptors (likely Sparse Textures or Descriptor Indexing).

Attempted Workarounds (All Unsuccessful)
Descriptor Fix: VKD3D_CONFIG=dx12_ignore_bad_descriptor_size (No effect).

Pipeline Stability: VKD3D_CONFIG=no_pipeline_library (No effect).

Vendor Forcing: force_vk_vendor=-1 & VK_ICD_FILENAMES pointing directly to intel_icd.x86_64.json.

Feature Level Limiting: VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0 (To avoid DX12 Ultimate instructions).

Hardware Isolation: Disabling Esync/Fsync to rule out CPU threading race conditions.

Notes for Developers (Intel/Mesa): the crash appears to be a GPU Hang triggered by the Dawn Engine's specific implementation of DX12 resource binding on Xe2 architecture. Logs suggest a mismatch in how the xe driver handles the transition between Media Foundation (video) and the D3D12 Render Loop. Possible regression or missing implementation in Mesa 26.x regarding Xe2-specific shader compiler paths. My specific card is a Shenzhen Gunnir Index B580, 12GB GDDR6 if that may eventually help you.

In conclusion: unplayable on Battlemage for now. Do not waste time with standard Proton "tricks" (spoofing or DX11 forcing); the fix must come from the Mesa/Kernel side or a specific vkd3d-proton quirk for the Xe2 instruction set.
